```{r tests-prompts_prepare}
    ),
    "User template not found",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
})

test_that('prompts_prepare works with domain role and domain parameter', {
  templates <- list(
    `system-domain` = file.path(prompts_dir, 'system-domain.md'),
    `user-domain` = file.path(prompts_dir, 'user-domain.md')
  )
  skip_if_not(all(file.exists(unlist(templates))))

  result <- prompts_prepare(
    role = "domain",
    templates = templates,
    language = "en",
    target = "artificial intelligence",
    text = "AI will replace all human jobs",
    target_type = "the object",
    domain = "technology and employment expert"
  )

  expect_type(result, "list")
  expect_true(nchar(result$system) > 0)
  expect_true(nchar(result$task) > 0)
})

test_that('prompts_prepare works with judger role and no additional options', {
  templates <- list(
    `system-judger` = file.path(prompts_dir, 'system-judger.md'),
    `user-judger` = file.path(prompts_dir, 'user-judger.md'),
    reference_resolution = file.path(prompts_dir, 'reference-resolution.md'),
    statement_resolution = file.path(prompts_dir, 'statement-resolution.md'),
    scale_guide = file.path(prompts_dir, 'guide-likert.md')
  )
  skip_if_not(all(file.exists(unlist(templates))))

  texts <- c("some text", "and more")

  result <- prompts_prepare(
    role = "judger",
    templates = templates,
    language = "en",
    add_reference_resolution = FALSE,
    add_statement_resolution = FALSE,
    target = "content analysis",
    text = texts,
    target_type = "the object",
    FavourResponse = "I have a feeling it is positive",
    AgainstResponse = "Most likely, this is some kind of malicious mockery",
    NeutralResponse = "Context is missing and we should be sceptical"
  )

  expect_type(result, "list")
  expect_true(nchar(result$system) > 0)
  expect_true(all(nchar(result$task) > 0))
  expect_length(result$system, 1)
  expect_length(result$task, length(texts))
  expect_no_match(result$system, "reference_instruction")
  expect_no_match(result$system, "statement_instruction")
  expect_no_match(result$system, "Resolve a reference if")
  expect_no_match(result$system, "Rules for Statements")
})

test_that('prompts_prepare works with judger role and reference resolution', {
  templates <- list(
    `system-judger` = file.path(prompts_dir, 'system-judger.md'),
    `user-judger` = file.path(prompts_dir, 'user-judger.md'),
    reference_resolution = file.path(prompts_dir, 'reference-resolution.md'),
    statement_resolution = file.path(prompts_dir, 'statement-resolution.md'),
    scale_guide = file.path(prompts_dir, 'guide-likert.md')
  )
  skip_if_not(all(file.exists(unlist(templates))))

  texts <- c("some text", "and more")

  result <- prompts_prepare(
    role = "judger",
    templates = templates,
    language = "en",
    add_reference_resolution = TRUE,
    add_statement_resolution = FALSE,
    target = "content analysis",
    text = texts,
    target_type = "the object",
    FavourResponse = "I have a feeling it is positive",
    AgainstResponse = "Most likely, this is some kind of malicious mockery",
    NeutralResponse = "Context is missing and we should be sceptical"
  )

  expect_type(result, "list")
  expect_true(nchar(result$system) > 0)
  expect_true(all(nchar(result$task) > 0))
  expect_length(result$system, 1)
  expect_length(result$task, length(texts))
  expect_no_match(result$system, "reference_instruction")
  expect_no_match(result$system, "statement_instruction")
  expect_match(result$system, "Resolve a reference if")
  expect_no_match(result$system, "Rules for Statements")
})

test_that('prompts_prepare works with judger role and statement resolution', {
  templates <- list(
    `system-judger` = file.path(prompts_dir, 'system-judger.md'),
    `user-judger` = file.path(prompts_dir, 'user-judger.md'),
    reference_resolution = file.path(prompts_dir, 'reference-resolution.md'),
    statement_resolution = file.path(prompts_dir, 'statement-resolution.md'),
    scale_guide = file.path(prompts_dir, 'guide-likert.md')
  )
  skip_if_not(all(file.exists(unlist(templates))))

  texts <- c("some text", "and more")

  result <- prompts_prepare(
    role = "judger",
    templates = templates,
    language = "en",
    add_reference_resolution = FALSE,
    add_statement_resolution = TRUE,
    target = "content analysis",
    text = texts,
    target_type = "the object",
    FavourResponse = "I have a feeling it is positive",
    AgainstResponse = "Most likely, this is some kind of malicious mockery",
    NeutralResponse = "Context is missing and we should be sceptical"
  )

  expect_type(result, "list")
  expect_true(nchar(result$system) > 0)
  expect_true(all(nchar(result$task) > 0))
  expect_length(result$system, 1)
  expect_length(result$task, length(texts))
  expect_no_match(result$system, "reference_instruction")
  expect_no_match(result$system, "statement_instruction")
  expect_no_match(result$system, "Resolve a reference if")
  expect_match(result$system, "Rules for Statements")
})

test_that('prompts_prepare works with judger role and both options', {
  templates <- list(
    `system-judger` = file.path(prompts_dir, 'system-judger.md'),
    `user-judger` = file.path(prompts_dir, 'user-judger.md'),
    reference_resolution = file.path(prompts_dir, 'reference-resolution.md'),
    statement_resolution = file.path(prompts_dir, 'statement-resolution.md'),
    scale_guide = file.path(prompts_dir, 'guide-likert.md')
  )
  skip_if_not(all(file.exists(unlist(templates))))

  texts <- c("some text", "and more")

  result <- prompts_prepare(
    role = "judger",
    templates = templates,
    language = "en",
    add_reference_resolution = TRUE,
    add_statement_resolution = TRUE,
    target = "content analysis",
    text = texts,
    target_type = "the object",
    FavourResponse = "I have a feeling it is positive",
    AgainstResponse = "Most likely, this is some kind of malicious mockery",
    NeutralResponse = "Context is missing and we should be sceptical"
  )

  expect_type(result, "list")
  expect_true(nchar(result$system) > 0)
  expect_true(all(nchar(result$task) > 0))
  expect_length(result$system, 1)
  expect_length(result$task, length(texts))
  expect_no_match(result$system, "reference_instruction")
  expect_no_match(result$system, "statement_instruction")
  expect_match(result$system, "Resolve a reference if")
  expect_match(result$system, "Rules for Statements")
})

test_that('prompts_prepare returns consistent results for same inputs', {
  templates <- list(
    `system-linguist` = file.path(prompts_dir, 'system-linguist.md'),
    `user-linguist` = file.path(prompts_dir, 'user-linguist.md')
  )
  skip_if_not(all(file.exists(unlist(templates))))

  result1 <- prompts_prepare(
    role = "linguist",
    templates = templates,
    language = "en",
    target = "content analysis",
    text = c("some text", "and more"),
    target_type = "the object"
  )
  result2 <- prompts_prepare(
    role = "linguist",
    templates = templates,
    language = "en",
    target = "content analysis",
    text = c("some text", "and more"),
    target_type = "the object"
  )

  expect_identical(result1$system, result2$system)
  expect_identical(result1$task, result2$task)
})

test_that('prompts_prepare produces different results for different target', {
  templates <- list(
    `system-linguist` = file.path(prompts_dir, 'system-linguist.md'),
    `user-linguist` = file.path(prompts_dir, 'user-linguist.md')
  )
  skip_if_not(all(file.exists(unlist(templates))))

  result1 <- prompts_prepare(
    role = "linguist",
    templates = templates,
    language = "en",
    target = "climate change",
    text = c("some text", "and more"),
    target_type = "the object"
  )
  result2 <- prompts_prepare(
    role = "linguist",
    templates = templates,
    language = "en",
    target = "artificial intelligence",
    text = c("some text", "and more"),
    target_type = "the object"
  )

  expect_identical(result1$system, result2$system)
  expect_disjoint(result1$task, result2$task)
})
```

## Tasks

```{r function-tasks_validate}
#' Validate task configuration
#'
#' @description
#' Internal helper to ensure that the generated tasks (user prompts) meet
#' the expected criteria after interpolation. It checks for correct length,
#' data type, and ensures no empty strings were produced.
#'
#' @param tasks A character vector of interpolated tasks.
#' @param expected_length The number of tasks expected (usually matching the number of input texts).
#'
#' @return Invisibly TRUE if validation passes or throws an error if tasks are not a character vector, have incorrect length, or contain empty strings.
#'
#' @keywords internal

tasks_validate <- function(tasks, expected_length) {
  rlang::try_fetch(
    ellmer:::check_number_whole(expected_length, min = 1),
    error = function(e) abort("Wrong {.arg expected_length} argument.")
  )

  if (any(is.na(tasks))) {
    abort("User prompt list contains missing values")
  }

  if (length(tasks) != expected_length || !is.character(tasks)) {
    abort(
      c(
        "User prompt interpolation returned unexpected results",
        "i" = "Consider checking variable placeholders in user templates"
      )
    )
  }

  if (any(nchar(tasks) == 0)) {
    abort(
      c(
        "User prompt is empty after interpolation",
        "i" = "Check that all required variables are provided"
      )
    )
  }

  invisible(TRUE)
}
```

```{r tests-tasks_validate}
test_that("tasks_validate returns invisible TRUE on valid input", {
  tasks <- c("Content analysis task 1", "Content analysis task 2")
  expect_true(tasks_validate(tasks, 2))
  expect_invisible(tasks_validate(tasks, 2))
})

test_that("tasks_validate throws error on incorrect length", {
  tasks <- c("Content analysis task 1", "Content analysis task 2")
  expect_error(
    tasks_validate(tasks, 3),
    "User prompt interpolation returned unexpected results",
    fixed = TRUE
  )

  expect_error(
    tasks_validate(tasks, 1),
    "User prompt interpolation returned unexpected results",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
})

test_that("tasks_validate throws error if tasks is not character", {
  expect_error(
    tasks_validate(list("Content analysis task 1", "Content analysis task 2"), 2),
    "User prompt interpolation returned unexpected results",
    fixed = TRUE
  )

  expect_error(
    tasks_validate(c(1, 2), 2),
    "User prompt interpolation returned unexpected results",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
})

test_that("tasks_validate throws error on empty strings", {
  tasks_with_empty <- c("Valid task", "")
  expect_error(
    tasks_validate(tasks_with_empty, 2),
    "User prompt is empty after interpolation",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
})

test_that("tasks_validate throws error on NA values", {
  # nchar(NA) is NA, which fails the if(any(nchar...)) check or behaves unexpectedly
  tasks_with_na <- c("Valid task", NA_character_)
  expect_error(
    tasks_validate(tasks_with_na, 2),
    "contains missing",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
})

test_that("tasks_validate works with single task", {
  expect_true(tasks_validate("Single task", 1))
})

test_that("tasks_validate rejects zero length expectation", {
  expect_error(tasks_validate(character(0), 0), "Wrong", fixed = TRUE)
})

test_that("tasks_validate rejects fractional `expected_length`", {
  expect_error(tasks_validate("A task", 1.5), "Wrong", fixed = TRUE)
})
```

```{r function-tasks_prepare}
#' Prepare chat objects and tasks for execution
#'
#' @description
#' Organises the final structure for parallel execution by cloning the base
#' chat object and pairing it with the corresponding system and user prompts.
#'
#' @details
#' This function handles two main scenarios for system prompts:
#' 1. A single system prompt applied to all tasks.
#' 2. A vector of system prompts matching the number of texts (e.g., when
#'    different domain experts are assigned to different texts).
#'
#' The function clones the `chat_base` for each system prompt to ensure
#' environment isolation during parallel processing. It also performs
#' validation on the user tasks using [tasks_validate()].
#'
#' @param chat_base An `ellmer::Chat` object used as a template.
#' @param prompts A list containing `system` (character vector) and
#'   `task` (character vector) prompts, typically from [prompts_prepare()].
#' @param n_texts Integer. The expected number of texts/tasks to process.
#'
#' @return A list with two elements:
#' - `chats`: A list of cloned and configured `ellmer::Chat` objects.
#' - `tasks`: A character vector of user prompts (tasks).
#'
#' @keywords internal

tasks_prepare <- function(chat_base, prompts, n_texts) {
  # General case when there may be > 1 system prompts
  ## (e.g. multiple domain roles)
  chats <- lapply(
    prompts$system,
    function(system_prompt) {
      # Each time clone the base chat
      chat <- chat_base$clone(deep = TRUE)
      # Set a system prompt
      chat$set_system_prompt(system_prompt)
      return(chat)
    }
  )

  if (length(chats) != 1 & length(chats) != n_texts) {
    abort(
      "Length of system and user prompts must match {.val {n_texts}}, \\
            got {.val {length(chats)}}"
    )
  }

  tasks_validate(prompts$task, n_texts)

  list(
    chats = chats,
    tasks = prompts$task
  )
}
```

```{r tests-tasks_prepare}
# Mocking a Chat object for testing
MockChat <- R6::R6Class(
  "MockChat",
  public = list(
    system_prompt = NULL,
    set_system_prompt = function(prompt) {
      self$system_prompt <- prompt
    }
  )
)

test_that("tasks_prepare returns correct structure for single system prompt", {
  chat_base <- MockChat$new()
  prompts <- list(
    system = "System Instructions",
    task = c("Task 1", "Task 2", "Task 3")
  )
  n_texts <- 3

  result <- tasks_prepare(chat_base, prompts, n_texts)

  expect_type(result, "list")
  expect_named(result, c("chats", "tasks"))
  expect_length(result$chats, 1)
  expect_length(result$tasks, 3)
  expect_equal(result$chats[[1]]$system_prompt, "System Instructions")
})

test_that("tasks_prepare returns correct structure for multiple system prompts", {
  chat_base <- MockChat$new()
  prompts <- list(
    system = c("System 1", "System 2"),
    task = c("Task 1", "Task 2")
  )
  n_texts <- 2

  result <- tasks_prepare(chat_base, prompts, n_texts)

  expect_named(result, c("chats", "tasks"))
  expect_length(result$chats, 2)
  expect_equal(result$chats[[1]]$system_prompt, "System 1")
  expect_equal(result$chats[[2]]$system_prompt, "System 2")
})

test_that("tasks_prepare throws error on mismatched system prompt length", {
  chat_base <- MockChat$new()
  # 2 system prompts for 3 texts is invalid (must be 1 or n_texts)
  prompts <- list(
    system = c("System 1", "System 2"),
    task = c("Task 1", "Task 2", "Task 3")
  )
  n_texts <- 3

  expect_error(
    tasks_prepare(chat_base, prompts, n_texts),
    regexp = "Length of system and user prompts must match 3",
    fixed = FALSE
  )
})

test_that("tasks_prepare validates user tasks using tasks_validate", {
  chat_base <- MockChat$new()
  # Empty task should trigger tasks_validate error
  prompts <- list(
    system = "System",
    task = c("Task 1", "")
  )
  n_texts <- 2

  expect_error(
    tasks_prepare(chat_base, prompts, n_texts),
    regexp = "User prompt is empty after interpolation",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
})

test_that("tasks_prepare clones the base chat deeply", {
  chat_base <- MockChat$new()
  chat_base$system_prompt <- "Original"

  prompts <- list(
    system = "New System",
    task = "Task 1"
  )

  result <- tasks_prepare(chat_base, prompts, 1)

  # Check that the clone has the new prompt but the base remains unchanged
  expect_equal(result$chats[[1]]$system_prompt, "New System")
  expect_equal(chat_base$system_prompt, "Original")
  # Verify they are different objects
  expect_false(identical(chat_base, result$chats[[1]]))
})
```

---

# Expert Analysis

## Prepare Expert Chats

```{r function-prepare_expert_chats}
#' Prepare chat configurations for expert agents
#'
#' @description
#' A high-level internal helper that orchestrates the preparation of expert
#' sessions for specific analysis roles (linguist, domain expert, or interpreter).
#'
#' @details
#' This function acts as a bridge between the input data and the execution
#' engine. It extracts necessary variables from the `inputs` list,
#' interpolates the corresponding templates using [prompts_prepare()],
#' and then initialises the chat objects via [tasks_prepare()].
#'
#' @param inputs A list containing all necessary data for prompt interpolation:
#'   - `texts`: Character vector of statements to analyse.
#'   - `targets`: Character vector of analysis targets.
#'   - `target_types`: Character vector of localised target types.
#'   - `prompt_templates`: List of paths to markdown template files.
#'   - `language`: Character string (e.g., "en").
#'   - `domain_roles`: (Optional) Character vector for the domain expert role.
#' @param chat_base An `ellmer::Chat` object used as a template for cloning.
#' @param expert_role Character. One of `"linguist"`, `"domain"`, or `"interpreter"`.
#'
#' @return A list containing:
#' - `chats`: A list of configured `ellmer::Chat` objects.
#' - `tasks`: A character vector of interpolated user prompts.
#'
#' @keywords internal
#' @importFrom rlang arg_match

prepare_expert_chats <- function(
    inputs,
    chat_base,
    expert_role = c('linguist', 'domain', 'interpreter')
) {
  expert_role <- rlang::arg_match(
    expert_role,
    c('linguist', 'domain', 'interpreter'),
    multiple = FALSE
  )

  # Load instructions for the expert
  prompts <- with(
    inputs,
    prompts_prepare(
      expert_role,
      templates = prompt_templates,
      language = language,
      text = texts,
      target = targets,
      target_type = target_types,
      domain = domain_roles
    )
  )

  tasks_prepare(chat_base, prompts, length(inputs$texts))
}
```

```{r tests-prepare_expert_chats}
# Reusing and slightly enhancing the MockChat
MockChat <- R6::R6Class(
  "MockChat",
  public = list(
    system_prompt = NULL,
    set_system_prompt = function(prompt) {
      self$system_prompt <- prompt
    }
  )
)

# Set-up common paths for tests
prompts_dir <- system.file('prompts', 'en', package = 'stancer')
if (!dir.exists(prompts_dir)) prompts_dir <- here::here('inst', 'prompts', 'en')

test_that("prepare_expert_chats successfully prepares linguist chats", {
  # 1. Prepare inputs list
  inputs <- list(
    texts = c("Statement 1", "Statement 2"),
    targets = c("Target A", "Target B"),
    target_types = c("statement", "statement"),
    language = "en",
    domain_roles = "linguistic expert",
    prompt_templates = list(
      `system-linguist` = file.path(prompts_dir, 'system-linguist.md'),
      `user-linguist` = file.path(prompts_dir, 'user-linguist.md')
    )
  )

  # Skip if files don't exist in the environment
  skip_if_not(all(file.exists(unlist(inputs$prompt_templates))))

  chat_base <- MockChat$new()

  # 2. Execute
  result <- prepare_expert_chats(inputs, chat_base, "linguist")

  # 3. Verify
  expect_type(result, "list")
  expect_named(result, c("chats", "tasks"))
  expect_length(result$tasks, 2)
  expect_length(result$chats, 1) # Single system prompt case
  expect_match(result$tasks[1], "Statement 1", fixed = TRUE)
  expect_is(result$chats[[1]]$system_prompt, 'character')
})

test_that("prepare_expert_chats successfully prepares domain chats", {
  inputs <- list(
    texts = "Statement about AI",
    targets = "AI",
    target_types = "object",
    language = "en",
    domain_roles = "AI ethics expert",
    prompt_templates = list(
      `system-domain` = file.path(prompts_dir, 'system-domain.md'),
      `user-domain` = file.path(prompts_dir, 'user-domain.md')
    )
  )

  skip_if_not(all(file.exists(unlist(inputs$prompt_templates))))

  chat_base <- MockChat$new()
  result <- prepare_expert_chats(inputs, chat_base, "domain")

  expect_length(result$tasks, 1)
  # Check if domain variable was correctly interpolated in system prompt
  # (Assuming the template uses {domain} placeholder)
  expect_match(result$chats[[1]]$system_prompt, "AI ethics expert", fixed = TRUE)
})

test_that("prepare_expert_chats throws error for invalid role", {
  inputs <- list(texts = "test")
  chat_base <- MockChat$new()

  expect_error(
    prepare_expert_chats(inputs, chat_base, "invalid_role"),
    regexp = "must be one of",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
})

test_that("prepare_expert_chats fails if inputs list is missing required elements", {
  # Missing 'texts' which is used in length(inputs$texts)
  inputs <- list(
    language = "en",
    prompt_templates = list()
  )
  chat_base <- MockChat$new()

  expect_error(prepare_expert_chats(inputs, chat_base, "linguist"))
})

test_that("prepare_expert_chats handles multiple system prompts (if prompts_prepare returns them)", {
  # This tests the logic flow through to tasks_prepare
  inputs <- list(
    texts = c("T1", "T2"),
    targets = c("A", "B"),
    target_types = c("object", "statement"),
    language = "en",
    domain_roles = c("Content analysis expert", "Statistician"), # Multiple roles
    prompt_templates = list(
      `system-domain` = file.path(prompts_dir, 'system-domain.md'),
      `user-domain` = file.path(prompts_dir, 'user-domain.md')
    )
  )

  skip_if_not(all(file.exists(unlist(inputs$prompt_templates))))

  chat_base <- MockChat$new()
  result <- prepare_expert_chats(inputs, chat_base, "domain")

  # If prompts_prepare returns 2 system prompts, tasks_prepare should return 2 chats
  # Note: This depends on how prompts_prepare handles vectorised 'domain'
  expect_named(result, c("chats", "tasks"))
  expect_type(result$chats, "list")
  expect_length(result$tasks, 2)
  expect_length(result$chats, 2)
  expect_is(result$chats[[1]]$system_prompt, 'character')
  expect_is(result$chats[[2]]$system_prompt, 'character')
  expect_match(
    result$chats[[1]]$system_prompt,
    "Content analysis expert",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
  expect_match(
    result$chats[[2]]$system_prompt,
    "Statistician",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
})
```

## Execute Role

```{r function-execute_role}
#' Execute expert role analysis
#'
#' @description
#' An internal worker that handles the execution of LLM requests for a
#' specific expert role. It switches between parallel execution
#' (when a single system prompt is shared) and sequential execution (when
#' each task requires a unique system prompt).
#'
#' @param expert_role Character. The role to execute (e.g., "linguist").
#' @param inputs A list containing texts, targets, and prompt templates.
#' @param chat_base An `ellmer::Chat` object used as a template.
#' @param info Character. A descriptive label for logging and error messages.
#' @param rpm Integer. Requests per minute limit for parallel execution.
#' @param verbose Logical. Whether to print progress messages to the console.
#'
#' @return A character vector of LLM responses, one for each input text.
#'
#' @keywords internal
#' @importFrom ellmer parallel_chat_text

execute_role <- function(
    expert_role,
    inputs,
    chat_base,
    info,
    rpm = 500,
    verbose = TRUE
) {
  tasks <- prepare_expert_chats(
    inputs,
    chat_base = chat_base,
    expert_role = expert_role
  )

  if (verbose) cat('    \U1F4CA', glue::glue('{info}...'), '\n')

  # If multiple system_prompts, e.g
  if (length(tasks$chats) > 1) {
    # Use sequential analysis
    results <- vapply(
      seq_along(tasks$tasks),
      function(task_i) {
        chat <- tasks$chats[[task_i]]
        result <- chat$chat(tasks$tasks[[task_i]], echo = 'none')
        if (!is.character(result)) {
          abort("Unexpected results in {info} {task_i}")
        }
        result
      },
      character(1)
    ) |> catch(tolower(info))
  } else {
    # Use parallel analysis
    results <- ellmer::parallel_chat_text(
      # Unpack the Chat object
      chat = tasks$chats[[1]],
      prompts = tasks$tasks,
      rpm = rpm
    ) |> catch(tolower(info))
  }

  if (!is.character(results) || (length(results) != length(inputs$texts))) {
    abort(
      c(
        "Unexpected results in {info}",
        "x" = "Input and output lengths differ: {length(inputs$texts)} vs {length(results)}"
      )
    )
  }

  results
}
```

```{r tests-execute_role}
# Reusing the MockChat
MockChat <- R6::R6Class(
  "MockChat",
  public = list(
    system_prompt = NULL,
    set_system_prompt = function(prompt) {
      self$system_prompt <- prompt
    },
    chat = function(..., echo = NULL) paste("Response", seq_along(list(...)))
  )
)

test_that("execute_role uses parallel execution when there is one chat object", {
  # 1. Set-up Mocks
  mockery::stub(
    execute_role,
    "prepare_expert_chats",
    list(
      chats = list(MockChat$new()), # Only 1 chat -> parallel
      tasks = c("Content Analysis Prompt 1", "Content Analysis Prompt 2")
    )
  )

  mock_parallel <- mockery::mock(c("Response A", "Response B"))
  mockery::stub(execute_role, "ellmer::parallel_chat_text", mock_parallel)

  inputs <- list(texts = c("T1", "T2"))

  # 2. Execute
  res <- execute_role(
    "linguist", inputs, MockChat$new(), "Testing Parallel", verbose = FALSE
  )

  # 3. Verify
  expect_equal(res, c("Response A", "Response B"))
  mockery::expect_called(mock_parallel, 1)
  # Check arguments passed to parallel_chat_text
  args <- mockery::mock_args(mock_parallel)[[1]]
  expect_equal(
    args$prompts,
    c("Content Analysis Prompt 1", "Content Analysis Prompt 2")
  )
})

test_that("execute_role uses sequential execution when there are multiple chats", {
  # 1. Set-up Mocks
  chat1 <- MockChat$new()
  chat2 <- MockChat$new()

  mockery::stub(
    execute_role,
    "prepare_expert_chats",
    list(
      chats = list(chat1, chat2), # 2 chats -> sequential
      tasks = c("Task 1", "Task 2")
    )
  )

  inputs <- list(texts = c("T1", "T2"))

  # 2. Execute
  res <- execute_role(
    "domain", inputs, MockChat$new(), "Testing Sequential", verbose = FALSE
  )

  # 3. Verify
  expect_equal(res, c("Response 1", "Response 1"))
  # For every Chat, there is only one response
})

test_that("execute_role throws error if output length is mismatched", {
  mockery::stub(
    execute_role,
    "prepare_expert_chats",
    list(chats = list(MockChat$new()), tasks = c("P1", "P2"))
  )

  # Parallel returns only 1 response for 2 inputs
  mockery::stub(execute_role, "ellmer::parallel_chat_text", "Single Response")

  inputs <- list(texts = c("T1", "T2"))

  expect_error(
    execute_role("linguist", inputs, MockChat$new(), "Error Test", verbose = FALSE),
    regexp = "Input and output lengths differ",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
})

test_that("execute_role prints progress message when verbose is TRUE", {
  mockery::stub(
    execute_role,
    "prepare_expert_chats",
    list(chats = list(MockChat$new()), tasks = "P1")
  )
  mockery::stub(execute_role, "ellmer::parallel_chat_text", "Result A")

  expect_output(
    execute_role(
      "linguist", list(texts = "T1"), MockChat$new(), "MyInfo", verbose = TRUE
    ),
    regexp = "MyInfo..."
  )
})
```

---

# Debate Stage

## Prepare Debater Chats

```{r function-prepare_debater_chats}
#' Prepare chat configurations for debate agents
#'
#' @description
#' Prepares the prompts and chat objects for Stage 2 (Debate). It takes the
#' linguistic, domain, and social media analysis results from Stage 1 and
#' injects them into the 'debater' templates.
#'
#' @param stance Character. The stance label to be debated for (used in prompts).
#' @param inputs A list containing:
#'   - `texts`, `targets`, `target_types`, `language`, `prompt_templates`
#'   - `analysis_results`: A list with `linguistic`, `domain`, and `social_media`
#'     vectors from Stage 1.
#' @param chat_base An `ellmer::Chat` object.
#'
#' @return A list with two elements:
#'   - `chats`: A list containing a single `ellmer::Chat` object.
#'   - `tasks`: A character vector of user prompts for each input text.
#'
#' @keywords internal

prepare_debater_chats <- function(
    stance,
    inputs,
    chat_base
) {
  # Prepare prompts using all context from Stage 1
  prompts <- with(
    inputs,
    prompts_prepare(
      'debater',
      language = language,
      templates = prompt_templates,
      text = texts,
      target_type = target_types,
      target = targets,
      stance = stance,
      LingResponse = analysis_results$linguistic,
      ExpertResponse = analysis_results$domain,
      UserResponse = analysis_results$social_media
    )
  )

  # Stage 2 requires a consistent system prompt across all items
  if (length(prompts$system) > 1) {
    abort(
      c(
        "Multiple system prompts are not allowed at stage 2",
        "i" = "Consider checking variable placeholders in system templates"
      )
    )
  }

  tasks_prepare(chat_base, prompts, length(inputs$texts))
}
```

```{r tests-prepare_debater_chats}
# Reusing the MockChat
MockChat <- R6::R6Class(
  "MockChat",
  public = list(
    system_prompt = NULL,
    set_system_prompt = function(prompt) {
      self$system_prompt <- prompt
    },
    chat = function(..., echo = NULL) paste("Response", seq_along(list(...)))
  )
)

test_that("prepare_debater_chats correctly passes Stage 1 results to prompts_prepare", {
  # 1. Set-up Data
  inputs <- list(
    texts = "Text 1",
    targets = "Target 1",
    target_types = "statement",
    language = "en",
    prompt_templates = list(t = "inst/prompts"),
    analysis_results = list(
      linguistic = "Linguistic Analysis",
      domain = "Domain Expert Analysis",
      social_media = "Social Media Analysis"
    )
  )

  # 2. Mock prompts_prepare to verify arguments
  mock_prompts <- mockery::mock(list(
    system = "System Prompt",
    user = "User Prompt"
  ))
  mockery::stub(prepare_debater_chats, "prompts_prepare", mock_prompts)

  # Mock tasks_prepare to return a standard structure
  mockery::stub(prepare_debater_chats, "tasks_prepare", list(chats = list(1), tasks = "T"))

  # 3. Execute
  prepare_debater_chats("negative", inputs, MockChat$new())

  # 4. Verify arguments passed to prompts_prepare
  args <- mockery::mock_args(mock_prompts)[[1]]
  expect_equal(args[[1]], "debater")
  expect_equal(args$stance, "negative")
  expect_equal(args$LingResponse, "Linguistic Analysis")
  expect_equal(args$ExpertResponse, "Domain Expert Analysis")
  expect_equal(args$UserResponse, "Social Media Analysis")
})

test_that("prepare_debater_chats aborts if multiple system prompts are generated", {
  # Mock prompts_prepare to return 2 system prompts
  mockery::stub(prepare_debater_chats, "prompts_prepare", list(
    system = c("System 1", "System 2"),
    user = c("User 1", "User 2")
  ))

  inputs <- list(texts = c("T1", "T2"), analysis_results = list())

  # Check for our custom abort message
  expect_error(
    prepare_debater_chats("con", inputs, MockChat$new()),
    regexp = "Multiple system prompts are not allowed at stage 2"
  )
})

test_that("prepare_debater_chats returns tasks_prepare output on success", {
  mockery::stub(prepare_debater_chats, "prompts_prepare", list(
    system = "Single System",
    user = "User Prompt"
  ))

  expected_output <- list(chats = list("MockChatObject"), tasks = "Final Prompt")
  mockery::stub(prepare_debater_chats, "tasks_prepare", expected_output)

  res <- prepare_debater_chats("neutral", list(texts = "T1"), MockChat$new())
  expect_equal(res, expected_output)
})

test_that("prepare_debater_chats fails with multiple system prompts", {
  mockery::stub(prepare_debater_chats, "prompts_prepare", list(
    system = c("System Prompt 1", "System Prompt 2"),
    user = "User Prompt"
  ))

  expect_error(
    prepare_debater_chats("positive", list(texts = "T1"), MockChat$new()),
    "Multiple system prompts are not allowed at stage 2",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
})
```

---

# judgement Stage

## Prepare Judger Chats

```{r function-prepare_judger_chats}
#' Prepare chat configurations for judge agent
#'
#' @param inputs Input texts
#' @param chat_base Base chat configuration
#'
#' @return List of chat configurations
#' @keywords internal

prepare_judger_chats <- function(
    inputs,
    chat_base
) {
  prompts <- with(
    inputs,
    prompts_prepare(
      'judger',
      language = language,
      templates = prompt_templates,
      text = texts,
      target_type = target_types,
      target = targets,
      FavourResponse = debate_results$positive,
      AgainstResponse = debate_results$negative,
      NeutralResponse = debate_results$neutral,
      # Additional instructions based on object types
      add_reference_resolution = 'object' %in% types,
      add_statement_resolution = 'statement' %in% types
    )
  )

  if (length(prompts$system) > 1) {
    abort(
      c(
        "Multiple system prompts are not allowed at stage 3",
        "i" = "Consider checking variable placeholders in system templates"
      )
    )
  }

  tasks_prepare(chat_base, prompts, length(inputs$texts))
}
```

```{r tests-prepare_judger_chats}
# Reusing the MockChat
MockChat <- R6::R6Class(
  "MockChat",
  public = list(
    system_prompt = NULL,
    set_system_prompt = function(prompt) {
      self$system_prompt <- prompt
    },
    chat = function(..., echo = NULL) paste("Response", seq_along(list(...)))
  )
)

test_that("prepare_judger_chats correctly passes Stage 2 results and flags", {
  # 1. Set-up Data
  inputs <- list(
    texts = "Text 1",
    targets = "Target 1",
    target_types = "statement",
    language = "en",
    types = c("statement"), # Should trigger add_reference_resolution
    prompt_templates = list(t = "file"),
    debate_results = list(
      positive = "Pos Debate",
      negative = "Neg Debate",
      neutral = "Neu Debate"
    )
  )

  # 2. Mock prompts_prepare to verify arguments
  mock_prompts <- mockery::mock(
    list(
      system = "System Prompt",
      user = "User Prompt"
    )
  )
  mockery::stub(prepare_judger_chats, "prompts_prepare", mock_prompts)
  mockery::stub(
    prepare_judger_chats,
    "tasks_prepare",
    list(chats = list(1), tasks = "T")
  )

  # 3. Execute
  prepare_judger_chats(inputs, MockChat$new())

  # 4. Verify arguments passed to prompts_prepare
  mockery::expect_called(mock_prompts, 1)
  args <- mockery::mock_args(mock_prompts)[[1]]
  expect_equal(args[[1]], "judger")
  expect_equal(args$target_type, "statement")
  expect_equal(args$FavourResponse, "Pos Debate")
  expect_equal(args$AgainstResponse, "Neg Debate")
  expect_equal(args$NeutralResponse, "Neu Debate")

  # Verify conditional flags
  expect_false(args$add_reference_resolution)
  expect_true(args$add_statement_resolution)
})

test_that("prepare_judger_chats aborts if multiple system prompts are generated", {
  mockery::stub(prepare_judger_chats, "prompts_prepare", list(
    system = c("Sys 1", "Sys 2"),
    user = c("User 1", "User 2")
  ))

  inputs <- list(texts = c("T1", "T2"), types = "statement", debate_results = list())

  expect_error(
    prepare_judger_chats(inputs, MockChat$new()),
    regexp = "Multiple system prompts are not allowed at stage 3",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
})

test_that("prepare_judger_chats correctly handles statement resolution flag", {
  inputs <- list(
    texts = "T1", targets = "A", target_types = "statement", language = "en",
    types = "statement", # Should trigger add_statement_resolution
    prompt_templates = list(),
    debate_results = list(positive = "P", negative = "N", neutral = "Neu")
  )

  mock_prompts <- mockery::mock(list(system = "S", user = "U"))
  mockery::stub(prepare_judger_chats, "prompts_prepare", mock_prompts)
  mockery::stub(prepare_judger_chats, "tasks_prepare", list())

  prepare_judger_chats(inputs, MockChat$new())

  mockery::expect_called(mock_prompts, 1)
  args <- mockery::mock_args(mock_prompts)[[1]]
  expect_false(args$add_reference_resolution)
  expect_true(args$add_statement_resolution)
})
```

---

# Pipeline Stages

## Stage 1: Parallel Analysis

```{r function-stage_1_parallel_analysis}
#' Execute Stage 1: Parallel expert analysis
#'
#' @description
#' Orchestrates the first stage of the analysis pipeline. It validates the
#' input data, then sequentially triggers three distinct expert analyses:
#' linguistic, domain-specific, and social media interpretation.
#'
#' @details
#' Although the function name contains "parallel", it refers to the
#' independent nature of the expert roles (they don't depend on each
#' other's output). Each role is executed via [execute_role()], which
#' may itself use parallel API requests.
#'
#' @param inputs A list containing all necessary data:
#'   - `texts`: Character vector to analyse.
#'   - `targets`: Character vector of targets.
#'   - `target_types`: Character vector of target types.
#'   - `prompt_templates`: List of template paths.
#'   - `language`: Character string.
#'   - `domain_roles`: Character vector for the domain expert.
#' @param chat_base An `ellmer::Chat` object used as a template.
#' @param verbose Logical. Whether to print stage progress and timing.
#' @param rpm Integer. Requests per minute limit for API calls.
#'
#' @return The `inputs` list with an additional element `analysis_results`,
#'   which is a named list containing character vectors:
#'   - `linguistic`: Results from the linguist role.
#'   - `domain`: Results from the domain expert role.
#'   - `social_media`: Results from the interpreter role.
#'
#' @keywords internal
#' @importFrom tictoc tic toc
#' @importFrom glue glue

stage_1_parallel_analysis <- function(
    inputs,
    chat_base,
    verbose = TRUE,
    rpm = 500
) {
  # Validate input structure
  required_fields <- c(
    'prompt_templates',
    'language',
    'texts',
    'targets',
    'target_types',
    'domain_roles'
  )
  validate_fields(inputs, required_fields, 'expert analysis')

  n <- length(inputs$texts)

  tictoc::tic('Stage 1')
  if (verbose) {
    cat(
      glue::glue("\U23F3 Stage 1: Expert analysis ({n} items)..."),
      "\n"
    )
  }

  expert_roles <- c('linguist', 'domain', 'interpreter')
  expert_info <- c(
    linguist = 'Linguistic analysis',
    domain = 'Domain expert analysis',
    interpreter = 'Social media interpretation'
  )
  analysis_results <- lapply(
    expert_roles,
    function(role) {
      execute_role(
        role,
        inputs = inputs,
        chat_base = chat_base,
        info = expert_info[[role]],
        rpm = rpm,
        verbose = verbose
      )
    }
  )
  names(analysis_results) <- c('linguistic', 'domain', 'social_media')

  # The result is a list of character vectors of length length(texts)
  ## One vector for each role
  inputs[['analysis_results']] <- analysis_results

  tictoc::toc(func.toc = stage_complete, quiet = !verbose)

  inputs
}
```

```{r tests-stage_1_parallel_analysis}
# Reusing the MockChat
MockChat <- R6::R6Class(
  "MockChat",
  public = list(
    system_prompt = NULL,
    set_system_prompt = function(prompt) {
      self$system_prompt <- prompt
    }
  )
)

test_that("stage_1_parallel_analysis validates inputs and returns results", {
  # 1. Set-up Data
  inputs <- list(
    texts = c("T1", "T2"),
    targets = c("A", "B"),
    target_types = c("statement", "statement"),
    language = "en",
    domain_roles = "AI Expert",
    prompt_templates = list(a = 1)
  )

  # 2. Set-up Mocks
  # Mock validate_fields to just pass
  mockery::stub(stage_1_parallel_analysis, "validate_fields", TRUE)

  # Mock execute_role to return role-specific strings
  mock_execute <- mockery::mock(
    c("L1", "L2"), # First call: linguist
    c("D1", "D2"), # Second call: domain
    c("S1", "S2")  # Third call: interpreter
  )
  mockery::stub(stage_1_parallel_analysis, "execute_role", mock_execute)

  # Mock tictoc to avoid console noise/errors
  mockery::stub(stage_1_parallel_analysis, "tictoc::tic", NULL)
  mockery::stub(stage_1_parallel_analysis, "tictoc::toc", NULL)

  # 3. Execute
  result <- stage_1_parallel_analysis(
    inputs,
    chat_base = MockChat$new(),
    verbose = FALSE
  )

  # 4. Verify
  expect_true("analysis_results" %in% names(result))
  expect_named(result$analysis_results, c("linguistic", "domain", "social_media"))
  expect_equal(result$analysis_results$linguistic, c("L1", "L2"))
  expect_equal(result$analysis_results$domain, c("D1", "D2"))
  expect_equal(result$analysis_results$social_media, c("S1", "S2"))

  # Verify execute_role was called exactly 3 times
  mockery::expect_called(mock_execute, 3)
})

test_that("stage_1_parallel_analysis fails if required fields are missing", {
  # Missing domain_roles
  incomplete_inputs <- list(
    texts = "T1",
    targets = "X",
    target_types = "statement",
    language = "en",
    prompt_templates = list()
  )

  # We don't stub validate_fields here because we want to test that it's actually called
  # and catches the error as expected.
  mockery::stub(stage_1_parallel_analysis, "validate_fields", validate_fields)
  expect_error(
    stage_1_parallel_analysis(incomplete_inputs, MockChat$new(), verbose = FALSE),
    regexp = "misses required fields: `domain_roles`"
  )
})

test_that("stage_1_parallel_analysis respects verbose flag", {
  inputs <- list(
    texts = "T1", targets = "A", target_types = "statement",
    language = "en", domain_roles = "AI Expert", prompt_templates = list()
  )

  mockery::stub(stage_1_parallel_analysis, "validate_fields", TRUE)
  mockery::stub(stage_1_parallel_analysis, "execute_role", list("Result"))
  mockery::stub(stage_1_parallel_analysis, "tictoc::tic", NULL)
  mockery::stub(stage_1_parallel_analysis, "tictoc::toc", NULL)

  expect_output(
    stage_1_parallel_analysis(inputs, MockChat$new(), verbose = TRUE),
    regexp = "Stage 1: Expert analysis"
  )

  expect_silent(
    stage_1_parallel_analysis(inputs, MockChat$new(), verbose = FALSE)
  )
})
```

## Stage 2: Parallel Debates

```{r function-stage_2_parallel_debates}
#' Execute Stage 2: Parallel agent debates
#'
#' @param inputs Input texts
#' @param chat_base Chat configuration
#' @param verbose Show progress
#' @param rpm Requests per minute limit
#'
#' @return Debate results
#' @keywords internal

stage_2_parallel_debates <- function(
    inputs,
    chat_base,
    verbose = TRUE,
    rpm = 500
) {
  # Validate input structure
  required_fields <- c(
    'prompt_templates',
    'language',
    'texts',
    'targets',
    'target_types',
    'analysis_results',
    'scale'
  )
  validate_fields(inputs, required_fields, 'stance debates')

  n <- length(inputs$texts)

  # Ensure Stage 1 results are present and valid
  validate_inputs(inputs$analysis_results$linguistic, n, 'Linguistic analysis')
  validate_inputs(inputs$analysis_results$domain, n, 'Domain expert analysis')
  validate_inputs(inputs$analysis_results$social_media, n, 'Social media analysis')

  tictoc::tic('Stage 2')
  if (verbose) {
    cat(
      glue::glue(
        "\U23F3 Stage 2: Parallel debates ({n} items × 3 stances)..."),
      "\n"
    )
  }

  stance_labels <- c('positive', 'negative', 'neutral')

  # 1. Prepare tasks for each stance
  debater_tasks <- lapply(
    stance_labels,
    function(stance_label) {
      prepare_debater_chats(
        stance = l(
          inputs$language,
          glue::glue('stance_{stance_label}'),
          inputs$scale
        ),
        inputs = inputs,
        chat_base = chat_base
      )
    }
  )
  names(debater_tasks) <- stance_labels

  # 2. Execute parallel debates for each stance group
  inputs[['debate_results']] <- lapply(
    debater_tasks,
    function(debater_task) {
      ellmer::parallel_chat_text(
        chat = debater_task$chats[[1]],
        prompts = debater_task$tasks,
        rpm = rpm
      ) |> catch('stance debates')
    }
  )

  tictoc::toc(func.toc = stage_complete, quiet = !verbose)

  inputs
}
```

```{r tests-stage_2_parallel_debates}
# Reusing the MockChat
MockChat <- R6::R6Class(
  "MockChat",
  public = list(
    system_prompt = NULL,
    set_system_prompt = function(prompt) {
      self$system_prompt <- prompt
    },
    chat = function(..., echo = NULL) paste("Response", seq_along(list(...)))
  )
)

test_that("stage_2_parallel_debates executes three debate branches", {
  # 1. Set-up Data
  inputs <- list(
    texts = c("T1", "T2"),
    targets = c("A", "B"),
    target_types = c("statement", "statement"),
    language = "en",
    scale = 'likert',
    prompt_templates = list(t = 1),
    analysis_results = list(
      linguistic = c("L1", "L2"),
      domain = c("D1", "D2"),
      social_media = c("S1", "S2")
    )
  )

  # 2. Set-up Mocks
  # mockery::stub(stage_2_parallel_debates, "validate_fields", TRUE)
  # mockery::stub(stage_2_parallel_debates, "validate_inputs", TRUE)
  mockery::stub(stage_2_parallel_debates, "tictoc::tic", NULL)
  mockery::stub(stage_2_parallel_debates, "tictoc::toc", NULL)

  # Mock l() localization helper
  mockery::stub(stage_2_parallel_debates, "l", function(lang, key, scale) key)

  # Mock prepare_debater_chats
  # We return a dummy chat and tasks
  mockery::stub(
    stage_2_parallel_debates,
    "prepare_debater_chats",
    function(stance, ...) {
      list(chats = list(MockChat$new()), tasks = paste(stance, "task"))
    }
  )

  # Mock parallel_chat_text to return stance-specific results
  mock_parallel <- mockery::mock(
    "Pos Result", # first call (positive)
    "Neg Result", # second call (negative)
    "Neu Result"  # third call (neutral)
  )
  mockery::stub(stage_2_parallel_debates, "ellmer::parallel_chat_text", mock_parallel)

  # 3. Execute
  result <- stage_2_parallel_debates(inputs, MockChat$new(), verbose = FALSE)

  # 4. Verify
  expect_named(result$debate_results, c("positive", "negative", "neutral"))
  expect_equal(result$debate_results$positive, "Pos Result")
  expect_equal(result$debate_results$negative, "Neg Result")
  expect_equal(result$debate_results$neutral, "Neu Result")

  # Verify it was called 3 times (once for each stance)
  mockery::expect_called(mock_parallel, 3)
})

test_that("stage_2_parallel_debates validates Stage 1 results before proceeding", {
  inputs <- list(
    texts = c("T1", "T2"),
    analysis_results = list(
      linguistic = "Too Short", # Should be length 2
      domain = c("D1", "D2"),
      social_media = c("S1", "S2")
    )
  )

  # Mock validate_fields but let validate_inputs run
  mockery::stub(stage_2_parallel_debates, "validate_fields", TRUE)

  # validate_inputs should throw error because linguistic length (1) != n (2)
  expect_error(
    stage_2_parallel_debates(inputs, MockChat$new(), verbose = FALSE),
    "Linguistic analysis returned unexpected results",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
})

test_that("stage_2_parallel_debates passes the correct RPM to parallel_chat_text", {
  # Set-up minimal valid inputs
  inputs <- list(
    texts = "T1", targets = "A", target_types = "statement",
    language = "en", scale = "likert",
    prompt_templates = list(t = 1),
    analysis_results = list(
      linguistic = "L1", domain = "D1", social_media = "S1"
    )
  )

  # Mock dependencies
  # mockery::stub(stage_2_parallel_debates, "validate_fields", TRUE)
  # mockery::stub(stage_2_parallel_debates, "validate_inputs", TRUE)
  mockery::stub(stage_2_parallel_debates, "l", "mock stance")
  mockery::stub(
    stage_2_parallel_debates,
    "prepare_debater_chats",
    list(chats = list(MockChat$new()), tasks = "task")
  )

  mock_parallel <- mockery::mock("Pos Result", "Neg Result", "Neu Result")
  mockery::stub(stage_2_parallel_debates, "ellmer::parallel_chat_text", mock_parallel)

  # Execute with custom RPM
  stage_2_parallel_debates(inputs, MockChat$new(), rpm = 42, verbose = FALSE)

  # Verify RPM in the first call to parallel_chat_text
  args <- mockery::mock_args(mock_parallel)[[1]]
  expect_equal(args$rpm, 42)
  expect_equal(args$prompts, "task")
})

test_that("stage_2_parallel_debates correctly localises stances for each branch", {
  # 1. Set-up data
  inputs <- list(
    texts = "T1", targets = "A", target_types = "statement",
    language = "en", scale = "likert",
    prompt_templates = list(t = "file"),
    analysis_results = list(
      linguistic = "L1", domain = "D1", social_media = "S1"
    )
  )

  # 2. Set-up Mocks
  # mockery::stub(stage_2_parallel_debates, "validate_fields", TRUE)
  # mockery::stub(stage_2_parallel_debates, "validate_inputs", TRUE)
  mockery::stub(stage_2_parallel_debates, "tictoc::tic", NULL)
  mockery::stub(stage_2_parallel_debates, "tictoc::toc", NULL)

  # Mocking l(), so that it returns a recognisable marker
  mockery::stub(stage_2_parallel_debates, "l", function(lang, key, scale) {
    paste0("loc_", key)
  })

  # Мокаем prepare_debater_chats, чтобы отследить, какой stance в неё пришел
  mock_prepare <- mockery::mock(
    list(chats = list(MockChat$new()), tasks = "task"), # для positive
    list(chats = list(MockChat$new()), tasks = "task"), # для negative
    list(chats = list(MockChat$new()), tasks = "task")  # для neutral
  )
  mockery::stub(stage_2_parallel_debates, "prepare_debater_chats", mock_prepare)

  # Mock parallel_chat_text to return stance-specific results
  mock_parallel <- mockery::mock(
    "Pos Result", # first call (positive)
    "Neg Result", # second call (negative)
    "Neu Result"  # third call (neutral)
  )
  mockery::stub(stage_2_parallel_debates, "ellmer::parallel_chat_text", mock_parallel)

  # 3. Execute
  stage_2_parallel_debates(inputs, MockChat$new(), verbose = FALSE)

  # 4. Verify
  # Достаем аргументы всех трех вызовов prepare_debater_chats
  mockery::expect_called(mock_prepare, 3)

  prep_args <- mockery::mock_args(mock_prepare)

  # Проверяем, что в каждом вызове аргумент stance был локализован правильно
  expect_equal(prep_args[[1]]$stance, "loc_stance_positive")
  expect_equal(prep_args[[2]]$stance, "loc_stance_negative")
  expect_equal(prep_args[[3]]$stance, "loc_stance_neutral")

  # Также можно проверить, что inputs и chat_base передаются без изменений
  expect_equal(prep_args[[1]]$inputs$language, "en")

  expect_equal(prep_args[[1]]$inputs$scale, "likert")
})

test_that("stage_2_parallel_debates handles empty input texts gracefully", {
  # If n=0, the loops should still work but return empty lists
  inputs <- list(
    texts = character(0), targets = character(0), target_types = character(0),
    language = "en", scale = "likert",
    prompt_templates = list(t = "file"),
    analysis_results = list(
      linguistic = character(0), domain = character(0), social_media = character(0)
    )
  )

  # mockery::stub(stage_2_parallel_debates, "validate_fields", TRUE)
  # mockery::stub(stage_2_parallel_debates, "validate_inputs", TRUE)
  mockery::stub(
    stage_2_parallel_debates,
    "prepare_debater_chats",
    list(chats = list(MockChat$new()), tasks = character(0))
  )
  # Mock parallel_chat_text to return stance-specific results
  mock_parallel <- mockery::mock(character(0), character(0), character(0))
  mockery::stub(stage_2_parallel_debates, "ellmer::parallel_chat_text", mock_parallel)

  result <- stage_2_parallel_debates(inputs, MockChat$new(), verbose = FALSE)

  expect_length(result$debate_results$positive, 0)
  expect_named(result$debate_results, c("positive", "negative", "neutral"))
})
```

## Stage 3: Parallel Judgement

```{r function-stage_3_parallel_judgement}
#' Execute Stage 3: Judge synthesis
#'
#' @description
#' Orchestrates the final stage of the analysis. It takes the results from all
#' three debate branches and uses a 'judge' agent to produce a structured
#' final stance judgement. This stage uses structured output to ensure the
#' results follow a predefined schema.
#'
#' @param inputs A list containing:
#'   - `texts`, `targets`, `types`, `target_types`, `language`, `prompt_templates`, `scale`.
#'   - `debate_results`: Results from Stage 2.
#' @param chat_base An `ellmer::Chat` object.
#' @param verbose Logical. Whether to print progress and timing.
#' @param rpm Integer. Requests per minute limit for API calls.
#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to `ellmer::parallel_chat_structured`.
#'
#' @return The `inputs` list with an additional element `judgement_results`,
#'   containing the structured stance analysis.
#'
#' @keywords internal
#' @importFrom tictoc tic toc
#' @importFrom glue glue
#' @importFrom ellmer parallel_chat_structured
#' @importFrom cli cli_warn

stage_3_parallel_judgement <- function(
    inputs,
    chat_base,
    verbose = TRUE,
    rpm = 500,
    ...
) {
  n <- length(inputs$texts)

  # Validate input structure
  required_fields <- c(
    'prompt_templates',
    'language',
    'texts',
    'targets',
    'types',
    'target_types',
    'debate_results',
    'scale'
  )
  validate_fields(inputs, required_fields, 'stance decision')

  # Ensure Stage 2 results are present and valid
  validate_inputs(inputs$debate_results$positive, n, 'Positive stance debates')
  validate_inputs(inputs$debate_results$negative, n, 'Negative stance debates')
  validate_inputs(inputs$debate_results$neutral, n, 'Neutral stance debates')

  tictoc::tic('Stage 3')
  if (verbose) {
    cat(glue::glue("\U23F3 Stage 3: Parallel judgement ({n} items)..."), "\n")
  }

  # 1. Prepare tasks for the judge
  judger_tasks <- prepare_judger_chats(
    inputs,
    chat_base = chat_base
  )

  if (verbose) cat("    \u2696\UFE0F Running parallel judgements...\n")

  # 2. Execute structured parallel judgements
  inputs[['judgement_results']] <- ellmer::parallel_chat_structured(
    chat = judger_tasks$chats[[1]],
    prompts = judger_tasks$tasks,
    type = type_stance_analysis(
      inputs$language,
      inputs$prompt_templates$scale_description,
      inputs$scale
    ),
    rpm = rpm,
    convert = TRUE,
    ...
  ) |> catch('making final judgement')

  # 3. Check for failed judgements (NA values)
  if (any(is.na(inputs$judgement_results$stance))) {
    n_na <- sum(is.na(inputs$judgement_results$stance))
    cli::cli_warn(
      "{n_na} item{?s} returned NA stance",
      call = rlang::expr(llm_stance())
    )
  }

  tictoc::toc(func.toc = stage_complete, quiet = !verbose)

  inputs
}
```

```{r tests-stage_3_parallel_judgement}
# Reusing the MockChat
MockChat <- R6::R6Class(
  "MockChat",
  public = list(
    system_prompt = NULL,
    set_system_prompt = function(prompt) {
      self$system_prompt <- prompt
    },
    chat = function(..., echo = NULL) paste("Response", seq_along(list(...)))
  )
)

test_that("stage_3_parallel_judgement executes structured chat with correct schema", {
  # 1. Setup Data
  inputs <- list(
    texts = c("T1", "T2"),
    targets = c("A", "B"),
    types = c("statement", "statement"),
    target_types = c("statement", "statement"),
    language = "en",
    scale = "likert",
    prompt_templates = list(scale_description = "Desc"),
    debate_results = list(
      positive = c("P1", "P2"),
      negative = c("N1", "N2"),
      neutral = c("Neu1", "Neu2")
    )
  )

  # 2. Setup Mocks
  # mockery::stub(stage_3_parallel_judgement, "validate_fields", TRUE)
  # mockery::stub(stage_3_parallel_judgement, "validate_inputs", TRUE)
  mockery::stub(stage_3_parallel_judgement, "tictoc::tic", NULL)
  mockery::stub(stage_3_parallel_judgement, "tictoc::toc", NULL)

  # Mock schema generator
  mockery::stub(stage_3_parallel_judgement, "type_stance_analysis", "mock_schema")

  # Mock prepare_judger_chats
  mockery::stub(stage_3_parallel_judgement, "prepare_judger_chats", list(
    chats = list(MockChat$new()),
    tasks = c("Task 1", "Task 2")
  ))

  # Mock parallel_chat_structured
  mock_results <- data.frame(
    stance = c("Strongly Disagree", "Agree"),
    reasoning = c("R1", "R2")
  )
  mock_parallel <- mockery::mock(mock_results)
  mockery::stub(
    stage_3_parallel_judgement,
    "ellmer::parallel_chat_structured",
    mock_parallel
  )

  # 3. Execute
  result <- stage_3_parallel_judgement(inputs, MockChat$new(), verbose = FALSE)

  # 4. Verify
  mockery::expect_called(mock_parallel, 1)
  expect_equal(result$judgement_results, mock_results)

  # Verify arguments passed to parallel_chat_structured
  args <- mockery::mock_args(mock_parallel)[[1]]
  expect_equal(args$type, "mock_schema")
  expect_true(args$convert)
})

test_that("stage_3_parallel_judgement warns when NA stances are returned", {
  inputs <- list(
    texts = "T1", targets = "A", types = "statement", target_types = "statement",
    language = "en", scale = "likert", prompt_templates = list(),
    debate_results = list(positive = "P", negative = "N", neutral = "Neu")
  )

  # mockery::stub(stage_3_parallel_judgement, "validate_fields", TRUE)
  # mockery::stub(stage_3_parallel_judgement, "validate_inputs", TRUE)
  mockery::stub(
    stage_3_parallel_judgement,
    "prepare_judger_chats",
    list(chats = list(1), tasks = "T")
  )
  mockery::stub(stage_3_parallel_judgement, "type_stance_analysis", "likert schema")

  # Return a result with NA stance
  mock_results <- data.frame(stance = NA_real_, explanation = "AI has failed")
  mockery::stub(
    stage_3_parallel_judgement,
    "ellmer::parallel_chat_structured",
    mock_results
  )

  # Expect warning from cli::cli_warn
  expect_warning(
    stage_3_parallel_judgement(inputs, MockChat$new(), verbose = FALSE),
    regexp = "1 item returned NA stance",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
})

test_that("stage_3_parallel_judgement passes additional arguments (...) to parallel call", {
  inputs <- list(
    texts = "T1", targets = "A", types = "statement", target_types = "statement",
    language = "en", scale = "likert", prompt_templates = list(),
    debate_results = list(positive = "P", negative = "N", neutral = "Neu")
  )

  # mockery::stub(stage_3_parallel_judgement, "validate_fields", TRUE)
  # mockery::stub(stage_3_parallel_judgement, "validate_inputs", TRUE)
  mockery::stub(stage_3_parallel_judgement, "type_stance_analysis", "likert schema")
  mockery::stub(
    stage_3_parallel_judgement,
    "prepare_judger_chats",
    list(chats = list(1), tasks = "T")
  )

  mock_parallel <- mockery::mock(
    data.frame(stance = "Strongly Agree", explanation = "AI Content Analysis")
  )
  mockery::stub(
    stage_3_parallel_judgement,
    "ellmer::parallel_chat_structured",
    mock_parallel
  )

  # Pass a custom argument like 'max_tokens'
  stage_3_parallel_judgement(
    inputs,
    MockChat$new(),
    verbose = FALSE,
    rpm = 111,
    max_active = 12,
    on_error = "stop"
  )

  args <- mockery::mock_args(mock_parallel)[[1]]
  expect_equal(args$max_active, 12)
  expect_equal(args$on_error, "stop")
  expect_equal(args$rpm, 111)
})
```

---

# Main Analysis Function

## LLM Stance Detection

### GENERIC FUNCTION

```{r function-llm_stance}
#' Detect stance using COLA framework
#'
#' @description
#' `llm_stance()` implements a multi-stage "Collaborative rOle-infused LLM-based
#' Agents" (COLA) framework to detect stance in text towards a specific target
#' or statement.
#' It supports character vectors and data frames (compatible with `mall` style).
#'
#' @param x Input text(s) as a character vector or a data frame.
#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to `ellmer::parallel_chat_structured`.
#'
#' @return A `stance_result` object (for character method) or a modified
#'   data frame (for data.frame method).
#'
#' @export
llm_stance <- function(x, ...) {
  UseMethod("llm_stance")
}
```

### CHARACTER METHOD

```{r function-llm_stance.character}
#' @rdname llm_stance
#' @description
#' The `character` method performs the full three-stage analysis:
#' 1. **Expert Analysis**: Linguistic, domain, and social media perspectives.
#' 2. **Stance Debates**: Parallel generation of arguments for, against, and neutral.
#' 3. **Judgement**: Final synthesis into a structured stance score.
#'
#' @details
#' ## Chat Configuration Recommendations
#' For optimal results, consider the following requirements for each stage:
#' \itemize{
#'   \item \strong{Expert Analysis (Stage 1)}: Requires excellent real-world
#'   awareness and reasoning. Structured output is NOT required.
#'   \item \strong{Stance Debates (Stage 2)}: Requires creativity and strong
#'   writing skills. Structured output is NOT required.
#'   \item \strong{Stance Judgement (Stage 3)}: Requires large context width,
#'   superior reasoning, and \strong{MUST} support structured output (JSON mode).
#' }
#'
#' @param target Character. The entity or statement the stance is directed at.
#' @param chat_base An `ellmer::Chat` object or a list of up to 3 such objects.
#' @param type Analysis type: "object" (entity-based) or "statement" (proposition-based).
#' @param language Language code (e.g., "en", "ru"). If `NULL`, auto-detected.
#' @param scale Stance scale: "categorical", "numeric", or "likert".
#' @param domain_role Optional specific role for the domain expert (e.g., "historian").
#' @param prompts_dir Optional path to custom prompt templates.
#' @param verbose Logical. Show progress and summary table.
#' @param rpm Integer. Global requests per minute limit.
#'
#' @return S3 object of class "stance_result" with:
#'   - summary: data.frame with results
#'   - analysis: expert analysis results
#'   - debates: debate results
#'   - judgements: final judgements
#'   - metadata: processing metadata
#'
#' ⁠@importFrom ellmer is_chat
#' @importFrom cli cli_abort cli_warn
#' @importFrom rlang is_character is_scalar_character is_list arg_match is_installed
#' @importFrom glue glue
#' @importFrom tibble as_tibble
#' @importFrom tictoc tic toc
#' @export
llm_stance.character <- function(
    text,
    target,
    chat_base,
    type = c('object'),
    language = stancer_available_languages(),
    scale = c('categorical', 'numeric', 'likert'),
    domain_role = NULL,
    prompts_dir = NULL,
    verbose = TRUE,
    rpm = 20,
    ...
) {
  ## Validation ----
  tictoc::tic('Analysis')

  # Validate text and target
  validate_character(text)
  validate_character(target)

  n <- length(text)

  # Validate type
  if (rlang::is_character(type)) {
    if (length(type == 2) != n) {
      type <- rlang::arg_match(type, c('object', 'statement'), multiple = FALSE)
    } else {
      type <- rlang::arg_match(type, c('object', 'statement'), multiple = TRUE)
    }
  } else {
    cli::cli_abort(
      "{.arg type} must be a character vector, got {.cls {class(type)}}"
    )
  }

  # Validate language
  if (is.null(language) || length(language) > 1) {
    if (rlang::is_installed("cld2")) {
      language <- cld2::detect_language(text[[1]], lang_code = TRUE)
      cli::cli_warn(
        "The analysis language was automatically detected: {.val {language}}"
      )
    }
  }
  if (rlang::is_scalar_character(language)) {
    if (!language %in% stancer_available_languages()) {
      cli::cli_abort(
        c(
          "Language {.val {language}} not available",
          "i" = "Use one of: {.val {stancer_available_languages()}}"
        )
      )
    }
  } else {
    cli::cli_abort(
      "{.arg language} must be a character scalar, got \
            {.cls {class(language)}} of length {.val {length(language)}}"
    )
  }

  # Validate scale
  scale <- rlang::arg_match(scale, c('categorical', 'numeric', 'likert'))

  # Validate domain_role
  if (is.null(domain_role)) {
    domain_role <- switch(
      language,
      uk = 'соціолог',
      ru = 'социолог',
      'social commentator'
    )
  } else {
    if (!rlang::is_character(domain_role) || length(domain_role) < 1) {
      cli::cli_abort(
        "{.arg domain_role} must be a character vector, got {.cls {class(domain_role)}}"
      )
    }
    if (!(length(domain_role) == 1 || length(domain_role) == n)) {
      cli::cli_abort(
        c(
          "{.arg domain_role} must have length 1 or {n} (same as {.arg text})",
          "x" = "Got {length(domain_role)}"
        )
      )
    }
  }

  if (length(domain_role) > 1) {
    cli::cli_warn(
      "Multiple domain roles detected. Parallel execution is unsupported."
    )
  }

  ### Chat Validation ----
  # Validate and arrange the `chat_base` argument across stages
  #
  # Recommendations (see vignette and docstrings):
  # 1. Expert analysis (Stage 1):
  #     - Excellent real-world awareness
  #     - Decent reasoning skills
  #     - Sufficient writing skills
  #     - Context width depends on task
  #     - Structured output: NOT required
  # 2. Stance debates (Stage 2):
  #     - Sufficient context width
  #     - Creative and writing skills
  #     - Structured output: NOT required
  # 3. Stance decision (Stage 3):
  #     - Large context width
  #     - Excellent reasoning skills
  #     - Excellent real-world awareness
  #     - Structured output: REQUIRED
  chats <- if (rlang::is_list(chat_base)) {
    # Validate each element
    for (i in seq_along(chat_base)) {
      if (!ellmer:::is_chat(chat_base[[i]])) {
        cli::cli_abort(
          "Element {i} of {.arg chat_base} is not an {.cls ellmer::Chat} object"
        )
      }
    }

    # Arrange based on list length
    indices <- switch(
      length(chat_base),
      `1` = c(1, 1, 1),
      `2` = c(1, 1, 2),
      `3` = c(1, 2, 3),
      cli::cli_abort(
        c(
          "{.arg chat_base} must have 1, 2, or 3 elements",
          "x" = "You provided {length(chat_base)}"
        )
      )
    )
    lapply(indices, \(i) chat_base[[i]]$clone(deep = TRUE))

  } else if (ellmer:::is_chat(chat_base)) {
    rep(list(chat_base$clone(deep = TRUE)), times = 3)

  } else {
    cli::cli_abort(
      c(
        "{.arg chat_base} must be an {.cls ellmer::Chat} object or \
                a list of 1-3 {.cls ellmer::Chat} objects",
        "x" = "Got {.cls {class(chat_base)}}"
      )
    )
  }

  ## Prompts ----
  prompt_templates <- templates_collect(prompts_dir, language, scale)

  ## Preparation ----
  target <- recycle_arg(target, n)
  type <- recycle_arg(type, n)

  target_types <- sapply(type, type_to_term, language = language)

  inputs <- list(
    texts = text,
    targets = target,
    types = type,
    target_types = target_types,
    language = language,
    scale = scale,
    domain_roles = domain_role,
    prompt_templates = prompt_templates
  )

  if (verbose) {
    cat("\n")
    cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
    cat(glue::glue("\U1F50D STANCE ANALYSIS - Processing {n} item(s)"), "\n")
    cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n\n")
    cat(glue::glue("Types: {paste(unique(type), collapse = ', ')}"), "\n")
    cat(glue::glue("Language: {language}"), "\n")
    cat(
      glue::glue("Domain roles: {paste(unique(domain_role), collapse = ', ')}"),
      "\n"
    )
    cat("\n")
  }

  ## Analysis ----
  output <- inputs |>
    stage_1_parallel_analysis(chats[[1]], verbose, rpm) |>
    validate_stage('Stage 1 (Expert analysis)') |>
    stage_2_parallel_debates(chats[[2]], verbose, rpm) |>
    validate_stage('Stage 2 (Stance debates)') |>
    stage_3_parallel_judgement(chats[[3]], verbose, rpm, ...)

  if (is.null(output$judgement_results) || nrow(output$judgement_results) != n) {
    cli::cli_abort("Final stance judgement returned unexpected results")
  }

  # Additional postprocessing of quantitative and Likert stance labels
  if (scale == 'numeric') {
    # output$judgement_results$stance <- truncate(
    #     (output$judgement_results$stance - 50) / 50,
    #     -1,
    #     1
    # )
  } else if (scale == 'likert') {
    output$judgement_results$stance <- factor(
      output$judgement_results$stance,
      levels = c(
        'Strongly Disagree',
        'Disagree',
        'Neutral',
        'Agree',
        'Strongly Agree'
      ),
      ordered = TRUE
    )
  }

  ## Postprocessing ----
  summary_df <- data.frame(
    text = text,
    target = target,
    target_type = type,
    language = language
  ) |>
    cbind(output$judgement_results) |>
    tibble::as_tibble()

  if (verbose) {
    cat("\U1F4CA Summary Table:\n")
    print(summary_df)
    cat("\n")
    cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
    toc <- tictoc::toc(func.toc = stage_complete, quiet = FALSE)
    cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n\n")
  } else {
    toc <- tictoc::toc(func.toc = stage_complete, quiet = TRUE)
  }

  ## Return ----
  structure(
    list(
      summary = summary_df,
      analysis = output$analysis_results,
      debates = output$debate_results,
      judgements = output$judgement_results,
      metadata = list(
        n_total = n,
        n_processed = nrow(output$judgement_results),
        n_failed = sum(is.na(output$judgement_results$stance)),
        language = language,
        types = unique(type),
        domain_role = domain_role,
        model = unique(sapply(chats, \(chat) chat$get_model())),
        elapsed = toc$toc - toc$tic,
        timestamp = Sys.time()
      )
    ),
    class = c("stance_result", "list")
  )
}
```

```{r examples-llm_stance.character}
# result <- llm_stance(
#   text = "I support renewable energy",
#   target = "renewable energy",
#   chat_base = chat_model,
#   type = "object",
#   scale = "categorical"
# )
```

```{r tests-llm_stance.character}
# Helper to stub all internal stages of llm_stance.character
stub_llm_stages <- function() {
  testthat::local_mocked_bindings(
    validate_character = TRUE,
    templates_collect = list(),
    validate_stage = function(x, ...) x,
    stage_1_parallel_analysis = function(x, ...) x,
    stage_2_parallel_debates = function(x, ...) x,
    stage_3_parallel_judgement = function(x, ...) {
      x$judgement_results <- data.frame(
        stance = "Neutral",
        explanation = "Mocked text mining result"
      )
      x
    }
  )
}

test_that('llm_stance.character is a function', {
  expect_true(inherits(llm_stance.character, 'function'))
})

test_that("llm_stance.character handles chat list and cloning", {
  # Mocking all internal stages to avoid API calls
  # stub_llm_stages()
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "validate_character", TRUE)
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "rlang::is_installed", TRUE)
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "cld2::detect_language", "en")
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "templates_collect", list())
  mockery::stub(
    llm_stance.character,
    "stage_1_parallel_analysis",
    function(x, ...) x
  )
  mockery::stub(
    llm_stance.character,
    "stage_2_parallel_debates",
    function(x, ...) x
  )
  mockery::stub(
    llm_stance.character,
    "stage_3_parallel_judgement",
    function(x, ...) {
      x$judgement_results <- data.frame(
        stance = "Negative", explanation = "Content analysis results")
      x
    }
  )
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "validate_stage", function(x, ...) x)

  # Use an empty Chat object
  chat <- ellmer::chat_mistral(echo = "none")

  # Test with single chat
  res <- llm_stance(
    "Text mining is fun", "Text mining", type = "object",
    chat_base = chat, verbose = FALSE, language = "en"
  )

  expect_named(
    res, c("summary", "analysis", "debates", "judgements", "metadata")
  )
  expect_equal(res$metadata$n_total, 1)
  expect_s3_class(res, "stance_result")

  # Test with list of 1 chat
  chat_list <- list(chat)
  res_list <- llm_stance(
    "Text mining is fun", "Text mining", type = "object",
    chat_base = chat_list, verbose = FALSE, language = "en"
  )
  expect_named(
    res_list, c("summary", "analysis", "debates", "judgements", "metadata")
  )
  expect_equal(res_list$metadata$n_total, 1)
  expect_s3_class(res_list, "stance_result")

  # Test with list of 2 chats (Stage 1&2 use chat 1, Stage 3 uses chat 2)
  chat_list <- list(chat, chat)
  res_list <- llm_stance(
    "Text mining is fun", "Text mining", type = "object",
    chat_base = chat_list, verbose = FALSE, language = "en"
  )
  expect_named(
    res_list, c("summary", "analysis", "debates", "judgements", "metadata")
  )
  expect_equal(res_list$metadata$n_total, 1)
  expect_s3_class(res_list, "stance_result")

  # Test with list of 3 chats (Stage 1&2 use chat 1, Stage 3 uses chat 2)
  chat_list <- list(chat, chat, chat)
  res_list <- llm_stance(
    "Text mining is fun", "Text mining", type = "object",
    chat_base = chat_list, verbose = FALSE, language = "en"
  )
  expect_named(
    res_list, c("summary", "analysis", "debates", "judgements", "metadata")
  )
  expect_equal(res_list$metadata$n_total, 1)
  expect_s3_class(res_list, "stance_result")
})

test_that("llm_stance.character aborts on invalid language", {
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "stancer_available_languages", c("en", "ru"))

  expect_error(
    llm_stance("text", "target", chat_base = list(), language = "fr"),
    regexp = "not available",
    fixed = FALSE
  )
})

test_that("llm_stance.character: language auto-detection and validation", {
  # stub_llm_stages()
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "validate_character", TRUE)
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "rlang::is_installed", TRUE)
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "cld2::detect_language", "en")
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "templates_collect", list())
  mockery::stub(
    llm_stance.character,
    "stage_1_parallel_analysis",
    function(x, ...) x
  )
  mockery::stub(
    llm_stance.character,
    "stage_2_parallel_debates",
    function(x, ...) x
  )
  mockery::stub(
    llm_stance.character,
    "stage_3_parallel_judgement",
    function(x, ...) {
      x$judgement_results <- data.frame(
        stance = "Negative", explanation = "Content analysis results")
      x
    }
  )
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "validate_stage", function(x, ...) x)

  # 1. Test auto-detection warning
  expect_warning(
    llm_stance(
      "Some English text",
      "Target",
      type = "object",
      chat_base = ellmer::chat_anthropic(echo = "none"),
      language = NULL,
      verbose = FALSE
    ),
    "automatically detected", fixed = TRUE
  )

  # 2. Test error for non-character language input (e.g. numeric)
  expect_error(
    llm_stance("T", "T", chat_base = MockChat$new(), language = 123),
    "must be a character scalar", fixed = TRUE
  )
})

test_that("llm_stance.character: chat_base list length validation", {
  # stub_llm_stages()
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "validate_character", TRUE)
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "rlang::is_installed", TRUE)
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "cld2::detect_language", "en")
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "templates_collect", list())
  mockery::stub(
    llm_stance.character,
    "stage_1_parallel_analysis",
    function(x, ...) x
  )
  mockery::stub(
    llm_stance.character,
    "stage_2_parallel_debates",
    function(x, ...) x
  )
  mockery::stub(
    llm_stance.character,
    "stage_3_parallel_judgement",
    function(x, ...) {
      x$judgement_results <- data.frame(
        stance = "Positive", explanation = "Text mining results")
      x
    }
  )
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "validate_stage", function(x, ...) x)
  chat <- ellmer::chat_mistral(echo = "none")

  # Error for 4 chats
  expect_error(
    llm_stance(
      text = "T",
      target = "T",
      language = "en",
      chat_base = list(chat, chat, chat, chat),
      verbose = FALSE
    ),
    "must have 1, 2, or 3 elements",
    fixed = TRUE
  )

  # Error for invalid object type
  expect_error(
    llm_stance(
      text = "T",
      target = "T",
      chat_base = "not a chat",
      language = "en",
      verbose = FALSE
    ),
    "object or a list of 1-3",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
})

test_that("llm_stance.character: domain_role length validation", {
  # stub_llm_stages()
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "validate_character", TRUE)
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "rlang::is_installed", TRUE)
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "cld2::detect_language", "en")
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "templates_collect", list())
  mockery::stub(
    llm_stance.character,
    "stage_1_parallel_analysis",
    function(x, ...) x
  )
  mockery::stub(
    llm_stance.character,
    "stage_2_parallel_debates",
    function(x, ...) x
  )
  mockery::stub(
    llm_stance.character,
    "stage_3_parallel_judgement",
    function(x, ...) {
      x$judgement_results <- data.frame(
        stance = "Positive", explanation = "Text analysis results")
      x
    }
  )
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "validate_stage", function(x, ...) x)

  # n = 2, but domain_role = 3
  expect_error(
    llm_stance(
      text = c("T1", "T2"),
      target = "Target",
      chat_base = ellmer::chat_mistral(echo = "none"),
      language = "en",
      domain_role = c("R1", "R2", "R3"),
      verbose = FALSE
    ),
    "must have length 1 or 2",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
})

test_that("llm_stance.character: likert scale post-processing", {
  # Custom stub for stage 3 to return a specific Likert value
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "validate_character", TRUE)
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "templates_collect", list())
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.character, "validate_stage", function(x, ...) x)
  mockery::stub(
    llm_stance.character,
    "stage_1_parallel_analysis",
    function(x, ...) x
  )
  mockery::stub(
    llm_stance.character,
    "stage_2_parallel_debates",
    function(x, ...) x
  )
  mockery::stub(
    llm_stance.character,
    "stage_3_parallel_judgement",
    function(x, ...) {
      x$judgement_results <- data.frame(
        stance = "Strongly Agree",
        explanation = "Likert scale result"
      )
      x
    })

  res <- llm_stance(
    text = "T",
    target = "T",
    type = "statement",
    chat_base = ellmer::chat_mistral(echo = "none"),
    language = "uk",
    scale = "likert",
    verbose = FALSE
  )

  expect_s3_class(res$summary$stance, "factor")
  expect_true(is.ordered(res$summary$stance))
  expect_equal(as.character(res$summary$stance), "Strongly Agree")
})
```

### DATA.FRAME METHOD

```{r function-llm_stance.data.frame}
#' @rdname llm_stance
#' @param data A data.frame containing (any of) `text`, `target`, `type`, `domain_role` or `language` arguments.
#' @param .output_col Name of the column where results will be stored.
#'
#' @importFrom cli cli_abort cli_warn cli_inform
#' @importFrom rlang is_null as_name
#' @export

llm_stance.data.frame <- function(
    data,
    text = NULL,
    target = NULL,
    type = NULL,
    domain_role = NULL,
    language = NULL,
    chat_base,
    scale = 'categorical',
    prompts_dir = NULL,
    verbose = TRUE,
    rpm = 20,
    .output_col = '.stance',
    ...
) {
  # Проверяем, что data это data.frame
  if (!is.data.frame(data)) {
    cli::cli_abort(
      "{.arg data} must be a data.frame, got {.cls {class(data)}}"
    )
  }

  # Tidy evaluation
  text_quo <- rlang::enquo(text)
  target_quo <- rlang::enquo(target)
  type_quo <- rlang::enquo(type)
  language_quo <- rlang::enquo(language)
  domain_role_quo <- rlang::enquo(domain_role)

  # Validate and get argument vectors
  text_vec <- catch_eval(text_quo, data)
  target_vec <-  catch_eval(target_quo, data)
  type_vec <- safe_eval(type_quo, data)
  language_vec <- safe_eval(language_quo, data)
  domain_role_vec <- safe_eval(domain_role_quo, data)

  # Fall-back to the defaults
  if (rlang::is_null(type_vec)) {
    type_vec <- c('object', 'statement')
  }

  if (rlang::is_null(language_vec)) {
    cli::cli_warn(
      c("Cannot find {.arg language} in data or environment")
    )
  }

  # Names for messages
  text_name <- rlang::as_name(text_quo)
  target_name <- rlang::as_name(target_quo)

  if (verbose) {
    cli::cli_inform(
      c(
        "i" = "Processing {.val {text_name}} and {.val {target_name}} columns"
      )
    )
  }

  result <- llm_stance(
    text = text_vec,
    target = target_vec,
    type = type_vec,
    language = language_vec,
    domain_role = domain_role_vec,
    chat_base = chat_base,
    scale = scale,
    prompts_dir = prompts_dir,
    verbose = verbose,
    rpm = rpm,
    ...
  )

  # Augment `data`
  n_rows <- nrow(data)
  n_results <- nrow(result$summary)

  if (n_results != n_rows) {
    cli::cli_warn(
      c(
        "Result has {n_results} rows, but data has {n_rows} rows",
        "i" = "Returning only matching rows"
      )
    )
    result$summary <- result$summary[seq_len(min(n_rows, n_results)), ]
  }
```
