```{r function-llm_stance.data.frame}
  data[[.output_col]] <- c(result$summary$stance, rep(NA, n_rows - n_results))

  attr(data, 'llm_stance_metadata') <- result$metadata

  if (verbose) {
    cli::cli_inform(
      c(
        "v" = "Analysis complete!",
        "i" = "Results added to {.val {(.output_col)}} column"
      )
    )
  }

  return(data)
}
```

```{r examples-llm_stance.data.frame}
# results <- llm_stance(
#   data = tweets_df,
#   text = "text",
#   target = "climate_change",
#   chat_base = chat_model
# )
```

```{r tests-llm_stance.data.frame}
test_that('llm_stance.data.frame is a function', {
  expect_true(inherits(llm_stance.data.frame, 'function'))
})

test_that('llm_stance.data.frame rejects wrong `data` argument', {
  expect_error(llm_stance(data = list()), "no applicable method", fixed = TRUE)
})

test_that("llm_stance.data.frame works with tidy evaluation", {
  df <- data.frame(my_text = "R is smart and tidy", my_target = "R language")

  # Mock the character method to return a dummy result
  mock_res <- list(
    summary = data.frame(
      stance = "Positive",
      explanation = "Text mining result"
    ),
    metadata = list(status = "ok")
  )
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.data.frame, "llm_stance", mock_res)

  res_df <- llm_stance(
    df, my_text, my_target, chat_base = list(), .output_col = ".stance",
    language = "en"
  )

  expect_true(".stance" %in% names(res_df))
  expect_equal(res_df$.stance, "Positive")
  expect_equal(attr(res_df, "llm_stance_metadata")$status, "ok")
})

test_that("llm_stance.data.frame: missing column and row mismatch", {
  df <- data.frame(band = "Kamelot")

  # 1. Missing column error
  expect_error(
    llm_stance(
      data = df,
      text = non_existent,
      target = band,
      type = "object",
      chat_base = list()
    ),
    "in data or environment",
    fixed = TRUE
  )

  # 2. Row mismatch warning
  mock_res <- list(
    summary = data.frame(
      stance = "Positive",
      explanation = "Text mining result"
    ),
    metadata = list(status = "ok")
  )
  mockery::stub(llm_stance.data.frame, "llm_stance", mock_res)

  df_long <- data.frame(
    txt = c("T1", "T2"),
    trg = c("A", "B")
  ) # 2 rows input

  expect_warning(
    df_stance <- llm_stance(
      data = df_long,
      text = txt,
      target = trg,
      language = "uk",
      chat_base = list(),
      verbose = FALSE
    ),
    "Result has 1 rows, but data has 2 rows",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
  expect_true(is.na(df_stance[[".stance"]][2]))
})
```

---

# Result Inspection and Display

## Print Method

```{r function-print.stance_result}
#' Print stance_result object
#'
#' @description
#' A custom print method for `stance_result` objects. It displays a formatted
#' header with processing metadata (items processed, language, models used, etc.)
#' followed by the summary table of stance results.
#' @param x A `stance_result` object.
#' @param ... Additional arguments to [base::print.data.frame()] or other `print` methods for the summary table.
#'
#' @return The input object (invisibly).
#' @importFrom glue glue
#' @export

print.stance_result <- function(x, ...) {
    cat("Stance Analysis Result\n")
    cat(strrep("=", 60), "\n")
    cat(
        glue::glue("Processed: {x$metadata$n_processed}/{x$metadata$n_total} items"),
        "\n"
    )
    cat(glue::glue("Failed: {x$metadata$n_failed} items"), "\n")
    cat(glue::glue("Language: {x$metadata$language}"), "\n")
    cat(glue::glue("Types: {paste(x$metadata$types, collapse = ', ')}"), "\n")
    cat(
        glue::glue(
            "Domain role(s): {paste(x$metadata$domain_role, collapse = ', ')}"
        ),
        "\n"
    )
    cat(
        glue::glue("Model(s) used: {paste(x$metadata$model, collapse = ', ')}"),
        "\n"
    )
    cat(glue::glue("Time elapsed: {round(x$metadata$elapsed, 2)} sec"), "\n")
    cat(glue::glue("Timestamp: {x$metadata$timestamp}"), "\n")
    cat(strrep("=", 60), "\n\n")

    cat("Summary Table:\n")
    print(x$summary, ...)

    invisible(x)
}
```

```{r examples-print.stance_result}
# result <- llm_stance(
#   text = "R is smart and tidy",
#   target = "R programming language",
#   chat_base = ellmer::chat_anthropic()
# )
# print(result)
```

```{r tests-print.stance_result}
# Set-up Mock Object
mock_res <- structure(
  list(
    summary = data.frame(
      text = "Julia is fast & beautiful",
      target = "Julia programming language",
      target_type = "object",
      language = "en",
      stance = "Positive",
      explanation = "Content analysis results"
    ),
    metadata = list(
      n_total = 1,
      n_processed = 1,
      n_failed = 0,
      language = "en",
      types = "object",
      domain_role = "sociologist",
      model = "gpt-4o",
      elapsed = 12.3456,
      timestamp = as.POSIXct("2026-04-30 15:30:00", tz = "GMT")
    )
  ),
  class = c("stance_result", "list")
)

test_that("print.stance_result outputs correct metadata and table", {
  # Verify Output
  # We check for key elements in the printed output
  expect_output(print(mock_res), "Stance Analysis Result", fixed = TRUE)
  expect_output(print(mock_res), "Processed: 1/1 items", fixed = TRUE)
  expect_output(print(mock_res), "Language: en", fixed = TRUE)
  expect_output(print(mock_res), "Model(s) used: gpt-4o", fixed = TRUE)
  expect_output(print(mock_res), "Time elapsed: 12.35 sec", fixed = TRUE) # Rounded
  expect_output(print(mock_res), "Summary Table:", fixed = TRUE)

  # 3. Verify Invisible Return
  # Using capture.output to suppress printing during the check
  expect_invisible(val <- print(mock_res))
  expect_equal(val, mock_res)
  expect_s3_class(print(mock_res), "stance_result")
})

test_that("print.stance_result handles multiple models and roles", {
  mock_res <- structure(
    list(
      summary = data.frame(
        text = c("Контент-анализ это важно", "Text mining это интересно")
      ),
      metadata = list(
        n_total = 2,
        n_processed = 2,
        n_failed = 0,
        language = "ru",
        types = c("object", "statement"),
        domain_role = c("социолог", "лингвист"),
        model = c("claude-3", "gpt-4o"),
        elapsed = 5.111,
        timestamp = as.POSIXct("2026-04-30 15:30:00", tz = "GMT")
      )
    ),
    class = c("stance_result", "list")
  )

  # Check collapsed strings
  expect_output(print(mock_res), "Types: object, statement", fixed = TRUE)
  expect_output(print(mock_res), "Domain role(s): социолог, лингв", fixed = TRUE)
  expect_output(print(mock_res), "Model(s) used: claude-3, gpt-4o", fixed = TRUE)
})
```

## Summary Method

```{r function-summary.stance_result}
#' Summarise stance_result object
#'
#' @description
#' Provides a statistical summary of the stance analysis results. It displays
#' the frequency distribution of detected stances and a cross-tabulation
#' of stances by target entity/statement.
#' @param object A `stance_result` object.
#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to [base::print.table()].
#'
#' @return The input object (invisibly).
#' @export

summary.stance_result <- function(object, ...) {
    cat("Stance Distribution:\n")
    print(table(object$summary$stance))
    cat("\n")

    cat("By Target:\n")
    print(table(object$summary$target, object$summary$stance), ...)

    invisible(object)
}
```

```{r tests-summary.stance_result}
test_that('summary.stance_result is a function', {
  expect_true(inherits(summary.stance_result, 'function'))
})

test_that("summary.stance_result outputs distribution tables", {
  # 1. Set-up Mock Object with multiple items
  mock_res <- structure(
    list(
      summary = data.frame(
        text = c("T1", "T2", "T3"),
        target = c("Target A", "Target A", "Target B"),
        stance = factor(
          c("Negative", "Neutral", "Negative"),
          levels = c("Positive", "Neutral", "Negative")
        )
      )
    ),
    class = c("stance_result", "list")
  )

  # 2. Verify Output Headers
  expect_output(summary(mock_res), "Stance Distribution:", fixed = TRUE)
  expect_output(summary(mock_res), "By Target:", fixed = TRUE)

  # 3. Verify Table Content
  # Check for stance counts in the distribution table
  expect_output(summary(mock_res), "Positive Neutral", fixed = TRUE)
  expect_output(summary(mock_res), "0        1        2", fixed = TRUE)

  # Check for target-based cross-tabulation
  expect_output(summary(mock_res), "Target A", fixed = TRUE)
  expect_output(summary(mock_res), "Target B", fixed = TRUE)

  # 4. Verify Invisible Return
  expect_invisible(val <- summary(mock_res))
  expect_equal(val, mock_res)
})

test_that("summary.stance_result handles empty or NA results", {
  mock_res <- structure(
    list(
      summary = data.frame(
        target = "Target A",
        stance = factor(
          NA_character_,
          levels = c("Positive", "Neutral", "Negative")
        )
      )
    ),
    class = c("stance_result", "list")
  )

  # table() by default excludes NA, so we expect 0 counts but the headers should remain
  expect_output(summary(mock_res), "Stance Distribution:", fixed = TRUE)
  expect_output(summary(mock_res), "Positive Neutral", fixed = TRUE)
  expect_output(summary(mock_res), "0        0        0", fixed = TRUE)
})
```

## Data Frame Conversion

```{r function-as.data.frame.stance_result}
#' Convert stance_result to data frame
#'
#' @description
#' Extracts the summary table from a `stance_result` object and returns it as
#' a data frame. This allows for easy further analysis using standard R tools.
#' @param x A `stance_result` object
#' @param row.names Optional NULL or a character vector giving the row names for the data frame. Missing values are not allowed.
#' @param optional Logical. If TRUE, setting row names and converting column names to syntactic names is optional.
#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to [base::as.data.frame()].
#'
#' @return Data frame
#' @export

as.data.frame.stance_result <- function(
    x,
    row.names = NULL,
    optional = FALSE,
    ...
) {
  x$summary
}
```

```{r tests-as.data.frame.stance_result}
test_that('as.data.frame.stance_result is a function', {
  expect_true(inherits(as.data.frame.stance_result, 'function'))
})

test_that("as.data.frame.stance_result extracts summary correctly", {
  # 1. Set-up Mock Object
  mock_summary <- data.frame(
    text = "R is smart and tidy",
    target = "R is good",
    type = "statement",
    stance = "Positive",
    explanation = "AI Content Analysis Reasoning",
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  mock_res <- structure(
    list(
      summary = mock_summary,
      metadata = list(n_total = 1)
    ),
    class = c("stance_result", "list")
  )

  # 2. Test conversion
  df_result <- as.data.frame(mock_res)

  expect_s3_class(df_result, "data.frame")
  expect_equal(nrow(df_result), 1)
  expect_equal(df_result$stance, "Positive")
  expect_equal(df_result, mock_summary)
})

test_that("as.data.frame.stance_result works with pipes", {
  # This is a common use case for users
  mock_res <- structure(
    list(
      summary = data.frame(a = 1:3, b = 4:6)
    ),
    class = c("stance_result", "list")
  )

  # Check if it behaves correctly in a functional chain
  res <- mock_res |>
    as.data.frame() |>
    nrow()

  expect_equal(res, 3)
})

test_that("as.data.frame.stance_result ignores additional arguments gracefully", {
  # Since our implementation just returns x$summary, we check it doesn't crash
  # if standard as.data.frame arguments are passed
  mock_res <- structure(
    list(summary = data.frame(a = 1)),
    class = c("stance_result", "list")
  )

  expect_no_error(as.data.frame(mock_res, row.names = "1", optional = TRUE))
})
```

## Inspect Method

```{r function-inspect}
#' Inspect stance_result contents
#'
#' @param x A `stance_result` object.
#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to methods
#'
#' @return Invisibly x
#' @export

inspect <- function(x, ...) {
  UseMethod("inspect")
}
```

```{r function-inspect.stance_result}
#' Inspect stance_result contents
#'
#' @description
#' A diagnostic tool to peer into the internal steps of the stance analysis.
#' It allows you to view metadata, raw linguistic/expert analysis,
#' internal debates between models, or the final explanation for a specific row.
#'
#' @param x A `stance_result` object.
#' @param what Character. Primary level: 'metadata', 'analysis', 'debates', or 'explanation'.
#' @param within Optional character. Secondary level (e.g., 'linguistic' for analysis).
#' @param index Integer. The row index of the text item to inspect.
#'
#' @return The input object (invisibly).
#' @export

inspect.stance_result <- function(
    x,
    what = c('metadata', 'analysis', 'debates', 'explanation'),
    within = NULL,
    index = 1
) {

  what <- match.arg(what)
  ellmer:::check_number_whole(index, min = 1)

  switch(
    what,

    #### Inspect Metadata ----
    metadata = {
      cat("\n", cli::rule("METADATA"), "\n\n")
      yaml::write_yaml(x$metadata, file = stdout())
    },

    #### Inspect Analysis Results ----
    analysis = {
      if (is.null(within)) {
        cat("\n", cli::rule("ANALYSIS - Available sections"), "\n\n")
        available <- names(x$analysis)
        cat("Available sections:\n")
        for (section in available) {
          cat(
            cli::col_cyan(
              sprintf("  • %s\n", section)
            )
          )
        }
        cat(
          "\nUsage: ",
          "inspect(result, what = 'analysis', ",
          "within = 'section_name')\n\n",
          sep = ""
        )
      } else {
        if (!within %in% names(x$analysis)) {
          cli::cli_abort(
            "Section '{within}' not found in analysis. ",
            "Available: {.val {names(x$analysis)}}"
          )
        }

        if (index > length(x$analysis)) {
          cli::cli_abort(
            "Index {index} out of bounds. ",
            "Analysis has {length(x$analysis)} rows."
          )
        }

        content <- x$analysis[[within]][index]

        cat(
          "\n",
          cli::rule(
            sprintf(
              "ANALYSIS: %s [row %d]",
              within,
              index
            )
          ),
          "\n\n"
        )

        if (is.na(content) || is.null(content)) {
          cat(cli::col_yellow("(empty)\n\n"))
        } else {
          cat(ellmer::interpolate(content))
          cat("\n")
        }
      }
    },

    #### Inspect Stance Debates ----
    debates = {
      if (is.null(within)) {
        cat("\n", cli::rule("DEBATES - Available sections"), "\n\n")
        available <- names(x$debates)
        cat("Available sections:\n")
        for (section in available) {
          cat(
            cli::col_cyan(
              sprintf("  • %s\n", section)
            )
          )
        }
        cat(
          "\nUsage: ",
          "inspect(result, what = 'debates', ",
          "within = 'section_name')\n\n",
          sep = ""
        )
      } else {
        if (!within %in% names(x$debates)) {
          cli::cli_abort(
            "Section '{within}' not found in debates. ",
            "Available: {.val {names(x$debates)}}"
          )
        }

        if (index > length(x$debates)) {
          cli::cli_abort(
            "Index {index} out of bounds. ",
            "Debates has {length(x$debates)} rows."
          )
        }

        content <- x$debates[[within]][index]

        cat(
          "\n",
          cli::rule(
            sprintf(
              "DEBATES: %s [row %d]",
              within,
              index
            )
          ),
          "\n\n"
        )

        if (is.na(content) || is.null(content)) {
          cat(cli::col_yellow("(empty)\n\n"))
        } else {
          cat(ellmer::interpolate(content))
          cat("\n")
        }
      }
    },

    #### Inspect Explanation ----
    explanation = {
      if (!is.null(within)) {
        cli::cli_warn(
          c(
            'Stance result explanation has no sections.',
            'Unused argument: {.arg within}'
          )
        )
      }

      if (index > length(x$summary$explanation)) {
        cli::cli_abort(
          c(
            "Index {index} out of bounds. ",
            "The data have {nrow(x$summary)} rows."
          )
        )
      }

      content <- x[['summary']][['explanation']][index]

      cat(
        "\n",
        cli::rule(
          sprintf("EXPLANATION: [row %d]", index)
        ),
        "\n\n"
      )

      if (is.na(content) || is.null(content)) {
        cat(cli::col_yellow("(empty)\n\n"))
      } else {
        cat(ellmer::interpolate(content))
        cat("\n")
      }
    }
  )

  invisible(x)
}
```

```{r examples-inspect.stance_result}
# result <- llm_stance(...)
# inspect(result, what = 'metadata')
# inspect(result, what = 'analysis', within = 'linguistic')
# inspect(result, what = 'debates', within = 'negative', index = 1)
```

```{r tests-inspect.stance_result}
test_that('inspect.stance_result is a function', {
  expect_true(inherits(inspect.stance_result, 'function'))
})

mock_res <- structure(
  list(
    summary = data.frame(
      text = c("Julia is fast & beautiful", "R is smart and tidy"),
      target = c("Julia programming language", "R programming language"),
      target_type = "object",
      language = "en",
      stance = factor(
        'Positive',
        levels = c('Positive', 'Negative', 'Neutral')
      ),
      explanation = c(
        "The explicit statement 'Julia is fast & beautiful'",
        "The explicit statement 'R is smart and tidy'"
      )
    ),
    analysis = list(linguistic = c("L1", "L2"), domain = c("D1", "D2")),
    debates = list(positive = c("P1", "P2"), negative = c("N1", "N2")),
    metadata = list(
      n_total = 2L,
      n_processed = 2L,
      n_failed = 0L,
      language = "en",
      types = "object",
      domain_role = c("Python coder", "computer scientist"),
      model = "claude 3",
      elapsed = 11.111,
      timestamp = as.POSIXct("2026-04-30 15:30:00", tz = "GMT")
    )
  ),
  class = c("stance_result", "list")
)

test_that("inspect.stance_result: metadata and basic structure", {
  # 1. Metadata
  expect_output(inspect(mock_res, what = "metadata"), "METADATA", fixed = TRUE)
  expect_output(inspect(mock_res, what = "metadata"), "model:", fixed = TRUE)

  # 2. Analysis sections list (within = NULL)
  expect_output(
    inspect(mock_res, what = "analysis"),
    "ANALYSIS - Available sections",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
  expect_output(inspect(mock_res, what = "analysis"), "linguistic", fixed = TRUE)

  # 3. Specific analysis content
  expect_output(
    inspect(mock_res, what = "analysis", within = "linguistic", index = 1),
    "ANALYSIS: linguistic [row 1]",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
  expect_output(
    inspect(mock_res, what = "analysis", within = "linguistic", index = 1),
    "L1",
    fixed = TRUE
  )

  expect_output(
    inspect(mock_res, what = "debates"),
    "DEBATES - Available sections",
    fixed = TRUE
  )

  expect_output(
    inspect(mock_res, what = "debates", within = "positive", index = 1),
    "DEBATES: positive [row 1]",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
  expect_output(
    inspect(mock_res, what = "debates", within = "negative", index = 2),
    "N2",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
})

test_that("inspect.stance_result: debates and empty content", {
  mock_res <- structure(
    list(
      debates = list(positive = c("P1", NA_character_))
    ),
    class = c("stance_result", "list")
  )

  # 1. Debates sections list
  expect_output(
    inspect(mock_res, what = "debates"),
    "DEBATES - Available sections",
    fixed = TRUE
  )

  # 2. Empty content (NA)
  expect_error(
    inspect(mock_res,what = "debates", within = "positive", index = 2),
    "out of bounds",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
})

test_that("inspect.stance_result: explanation and warnings", {
  # 1. Explanation content
  expect_output(
    inspect(mock_res, what = "explanation"),
    "EXPLANATION: [row 1]",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
  expect_output(
    inspect(mock_res, what = "explanation"),
    "The explicit statement",
    fixed = TRUE
  )

  # 2. Warning when 'within' is provided for explanation
  expect_warning(
    inspect(mock_res, what = "explanation", within = "ignored"),
    "has no sections"
  )
})

test_that("inspect.stance_result: error handling (out of bounds and missing sections)", {
  # 1. Invalid 'within' section
  expect_error(
    inspect(mock_res, what = "analysis", within = "wrong"),
    "not found in analysis",
    fixed = TRUE
  )
  expect_error(
    inspect(mock_res, what = "debates", within = "wrong"),
    "not found in debates",
    fixed = TRUE
  )

  # 2. Index out of bounds
  expect_error(
    inspect(mock_res, what = "analysis", within = "linguistic", index = 99),
    "out of bounds"
  )
  expect_error(
    inspect(mock_res, what = "explanation", index = 99),
    "out of bounds"
  )
  expect_error(
    inspect(mock_res, what = "debates", within = "positive", index = 99),
    "out of bounds"
  )

  # 3. Invalid index type
  expect_error(inspect(mock_res, index = 0), "must be a whole number")
})
```

---

# Inflate

```{r development-inflate, eval=FALSE}
fusen::inflate(
  pkg = here::here(),
  flat_file = here::here('dev', 'flat_stancer.Rmd'),
  codecov = TRUE,
  clean = TRUE,
  vignette_name = "Get started"
)
```

```{r development-document, eval = FALSE}
devtools::document()
devtools::build_readme()
```
